AI Security Services

The Future is AI.
So are the Threats.

Cybertronium hardens your LLMs, AI models, agents and AI management systems against adversarial AI, autonomous attacker swarms and emerging global regulation — from pentest to ISO 42001 certification.

OWASP LLM Top 10
MITRE ATLAS
ISO/IEC 42001
EU AI Act
Book an AI Risk Briefing
77%
Of enterprises run GenAI without an AI security program
10×
Faster adversarial AI attack iteration vs human red teams

Average cost of an AI/ML-related data breach
ISO 42001
First global AI management system standard

The Age of Adversarial AI

Why AI Security can't wait

Attackers now ship autonomous agent swarms that probe, exploit and exfiltrate at machine speed. Every prompt, model and agent in your stack is a new attack surface — and most defenders haven't even inventoried theirs.

Adversarial AI

Attackers weaponise LLMs to craft polymorphic malware, evade detection and generate hyper-personalised social engineering at scale.

AI Predator Swarms

Autonomous agent swarms probe, exploit and pivot across your AI surface 24x7 — no coffee breaks, no fatigue, no mercy.

Prompt Injection & Jailbreaks

Indirect prompt injection, jailbreaks and tool-misuse hijack your AI agents into leaking secrets or executing attacker intent.

Model Supply Chain Risk

Poisoned weights, trojaned fine-tunes and compromised MCP / plugin ecosystems silently corrupt the models you depend on.

Data Leakage via LLMs

Sensitive PII, IP and credentials bleed out through prompts, embeddings, RAG indexes and overly-permissive agent actions.

Regulatory Exposure

EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001, MAS FEAT, RBI and SEBI mandates are converging — unprepared enterprises face fines and bans.

By the Numbers

Traditional AppSec ≠ AI Security

Adversaries move at machine speed. Most security operations still rely on human-speed workflows, fragmented tools and pay-per-gigabyte SIEMs.

Coverage of AI-specific threats

95%
Cybertronium AI Security
22%
Traditional AppSec / WAF
38%
Generic LLM guardrails

Time-to-exploit by attacker type

100%
Autonomous AI swarms
35%
Skilled human red team
18%
Opportunistic attackers

Sources: OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025), MITRE ATLAS case studies, Gartner AI TRiSM Hype Cycle, IBM Cost of a Data Breach

Cybertronium AI Security Services

From offensive testing to AI governance

Four integrated services that secure the full AI lifecycle — model, agent, data and management system.

01
Offensive

LLM, AI Models & AI Agents Penetration Testing

Deep, manual penetration testing of your LLM apps, custom models, RAG pipelines and agentic systems — aligned to OWASP LLM Top 10, MITRE ATLAS and NIST AI RMF.

  • Prompt injection (direct & indirect), jailbreaks, system prompt extraction
  • Tool / function-call abuse and MCP plugin exploitation
  • Training data extraction, membership inference, model inversion
  • Insecure output handling, SSRF, RCE via AI-generated code
  • Agent goal hijacking, recursive loop abuse and resource exhaustion
02
Adversarial

AI Red Team

Continuous, scenario-driven adversarial emulation against your AI surface — simulating real-world threat actors and autonomous attacker swarms.

  • Adversarial ML: evasion, poisoning, backdoor and transfer attacks
  • Multi-turn jailbreak chains and policy-bypass campaigns
  • Autonomous red-team agents that learn and adapt across engagements
  • Bias, toxicity, hallucination and misuse stress-testing
  • Executive reporting mapped to MITRE ATLAS tactics & techniques
03
Assurance

AI Assurance & Governance

End-to-end governance, risk and assurance programmes that make your AI defensible to boards, regulators and customers.

  • AI inventory, risk classification and impact assessments
  • Model cards, system cards, data sheets and lineage tracking
  • Policy, RACI and human-in-the-loop control design
  • Bias, fairness, explainability and robustness evaluation
  • Third-party model & vendor AI risk due diligence
04
Compliance

AI Management System (ISO/IEC 42001) Consulting

Implement and certify the world's first AI management system standard — aligned with EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 23894 and sector regulators.

  • Gap assessment against ISO/IEC 42001 controls and Annex A
  • AIMS scope, context, leadership and risk methodology design
  • Documented policies, procedures and Statement of Applicability
  • Internal audit, management review and certification readiness
  • Integration with existing ISO 27001 / 27701 / 9001 systems

Our Approach

The AI Security lifecycle

Discover

Inventory every model, agent, dataset, prompt and plugin across your estate.

Assess

Pentest, red-team and risk-assess against OWASP LLM, MITRE ATLAS and ISO 42001.

Govern

Stand up the AI Management System: policies, controls, roles and evidence.

Defend

Deploy AI-SOC monitoring, guardrails and adaptive response playbooks.

Improve

Continuous adversarial testing, metrics and board-level assurance reporting.

Mapped to Global Frameworks
